title: STANDARD FOR MILKFAT PRODUCTS CXS 280-1973 Formerly CODEX STAN A-2-1973. Adopted in 1973. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR MILKFAT PRODUCTS CXS 280-1973 Formerly CODEX STAN A-2-1973. Adopted in 1973. Revised in 1999. Amended in 2006, 2010, 2018, 2022.
sections.summary: Title and identification of the Codex Standard for Milkfat Products (CXS 280-1973), showing adoption in 1973, revisions in 1999 and amendments in 2006, 2010, 2018 and 2022.

sections.section: ## 2022 Amendment The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty-fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
sections.summary: Records the 2022 amendment decided at the 45th Codex Alimentarius Commission, which replaces the previous Section 7.2 text on non-retail container labelling with a requirement to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Scope: applies to Anhydrous Milkfat, Milkfat, Anhydrous Butter oil, Butter oil and Ghee intended for further processing or culinary use, in accordance with the product description in Section 2.

Defines Anhydrous Milkfat, Milkfat, Anhydrous Butter oil and Butter oil as fatty products derived exclusively from milk by processes removing almost all water and non‑fat solids, and defines Ghee as similarly derived but with an especially developed flavour and physical structure.

Sets essential composition and quality: raw material is milk or milk products, permitted ingredient is starter cultures of harmless lactic acid bacteria, and minimum milkfat and maximum water requirements are specified for the products (see composition table).
Raw materials: milk and/or products obtained from milk.
Permitted ingredients: allows starter cultures of harmless lactic acid‑producing bacteria.

Composition table: minimum milkfat is 99.8% m/m for Anhydrous milkfat/Anhydrous butter oil and 99.6% m/m for Milkfat, Butter oil and Ghee; maximum water is 0.1% m/m for Anhydrous milkfat/Anhydrous butter oil (water not specified for the others).

Food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for Food Category 02.1.1 (Butter oil, anhydrous milkfat, ghee) may be used, and inert gas flushing of airtight containers before, during and after filling is permitted.

sections.section: 4.1 Inert gas with which airtight containers are flushed before, during and after filling with product
sections.summary: Permits the use of inert gas to flush airtight containers before, during and after filling with the product (packaging practice).

Requires the products to comply with maximum contaminant and toxin levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995), and requires milk used to meet those milk-specific contaminant/toxin limits and CAC-established maximum residue limits for veterinary drugs and pesticides in milk.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and relevant Codex texts, and compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Labelling: in addition to general labelling and dairy terms standards, specifies permissible product names per Sections 2 and 3 and requires non‑retail container labelling to follow CXS 346-2021.

Specifies the name of the food shall be one of: Anhydrous milkfat, Milkfat, Anhydrous butter oil, Butter oil or Ghee, consistent with the product description, composition and any permitted antioxidants.

sections.section: 7.1 The name of the food shall be:
sections.summary: Lists permitted product names: Anhydrous milkfat, Milkfat, Anhydrous butter oil, Butter oil and Ghee.

Labelling provisions (reiterated): complements general prepackaged foods and dairy terms standards, prescribes the product names per Sections 2 and 3 and references CXS 346-2021 for non‑retail container labelling.
For compliance testing, sampling and analytical methods in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard shall be used.

Appendix (non‑normative): supplies additional information that does not change mandatory provisions, including extra quality factor tables, heavy metal limits and a reference to CXS 234-1999 for analysis methods.

Other quality factors table: maximum free fatty acids are 0.3% m/m for Anhydrous milkfat/Anhydrous butter oil and 0.4% m/m for Milkfat, Butter oil and Ghee; maximum peroxide values are 0.3 meq O2/kg fat for Anhydrous and 0.6 meq O2/kg fat for the others; taste/odour must be acceptable after heating to 40–45°C and texture described as smooth to liquid depending on temperature.
Other contaminants – heavy metals: maximum levels for the listed products are Copper 0.05 mg/kg and Iron 0.2 mg/kg.
Specifies maximum heavy metal limits for Anhydrous Milkfat, Milkfat, Anhydrous Butter oil, Butter oil and Ghee: Copper maximum 0.05 mg/kg and Iron maximum 0.2 mg/kg.
Non-substantive reference directing users to methods in CXS 234-1999 for other analytical procedures.
